All green blocks are gone (0 remaining), but the level isn't completed yet. Looking at the image, I can see:
- The green block row now has only the top row remaining (one block at the top center)
- Wait, looking more carefully, there's still one green block visible at the top of the former green block area
- The purple targets are still there at the top
- The agent is still at col 50-51
Let me check what objects remain. Maybe I need to click on the purple targets now, or navigate the agent somewhere.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, I see one green block remaining at the top center position. Let me check the segmentation to see what's left.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# All 25-pixel green blocks removed, but level not complete
# Check what remains

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")

# Check for remaining green blocks of any size
green_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
print(f"\nGreen objects: {len(green_blocks)}")
for n in green_blocks:
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The green blocks are gone, but there are now light green (N) objects in their place. Let me look at these:
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=25-29, cols=31-35 (top row, center)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=13-17 (bottom row, left)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=19-23 (bottom row, left-center)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=25-29 (bottom row, center)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=37-41 (bottom row, right-center)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=43-47 (bottom row, right)
- color=N, pixels=21, rows=31-35, cols=49-53 (bottom row, far right)
So there are 7 light green (N) objects remaining. Each has 21 pixels (was 25 for green blocks). The original green blocks were 5x5=25 pixels. Now they're 21 pixels, meaning 4 pixels were removed from each.
Looking at the layout:
- Top row: one N block at cols 31-35 (this was the one I clicked first, so it's different)
- Bottom row: 6 N blocks at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53
Wait, the original layout had:
- Top row: 7 green blocks at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53
- Bottom row: 7 green blocks at same columns
After clicking, the green blocks turned into N (light green) blocks with 21 pixels each. So I didn't actually remove them - I just changed their color!
But wait, the code said "Green blocks remaining: 0" because I was checking for color='g'. The blocks changed from green (g) to light green (N).
So the goal might be to click on all these N blocks too, or to click them in a specific order. Or maybe clicking them again will remove them completely.
Let me try clicking on the N blocks to see if they disappear or change further.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green blocks (g) changed to light green (N) blocks with 21 pixels each
# 7 N blocks remaining - need to click them too?

# Try clicking on all N blocks to see if they disappear
n_blocks = [n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 21]
print(f"N blocks: {len(n_blocks)}")

# Get center of each N block
centers = []
for n in n_blocks:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    center_row = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
    center_col = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
    centers.append((center_row, center_col))
    print(f"  rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, center=({center_row}, {center_col})")

# Click all N blocks
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in centers]
result = action(actions)
print(f"\nAfter clicking N blocks: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_remaining = sum(1 for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 21)
print(f"N blocks remaining: {n_remaining}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
